title: 9 Best Herbal Teas For Inflammation
intro: Herbal teas for inflammation are a type of non-pharmacological remedy that utilizes plant-based ingredients to alleviate symptoms associated with inflammation. These teas can treat inflammation due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which work by reducing oxidative stress and inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. Examples of herbal teas that can help treat inflammation include ginger tea, which has anti-inflammatory compounds like gingerol and shogaol that reduce pain and swelling; turmeric tea, which contains curcumin that has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects; willow bark tea, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that reduces inflammation and pain; and peppermint tea, which has anti-inflammatory compounds like menthol and menthone that reduce inflammation and improve circulation. Additionally, other herbal teas like chamomile tea, rosemary tea, and licorice root tea also exhibit anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms of inflammation.

preparations.herb_desc: Curcuma longa teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation and various inflammatory-related ailments. This herbal preparation contains bioactive constituents such as curcuminoids, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Curcuma longa teas also possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to alleviate symptoms associated with inflammation, including pain, swelling, and redness. The benefits of consuming Curcuma longa teas to treat inflammation include reduced symptoms, improved joint mobility, and a lower risk of chronic diseases, such as arthritis and cardiovascular disease.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1/2 teaspoon of dried turmeric root (Curcuma longa)
Boil the water in a pot and then reduce heat to a simmer
Add the dried turmeric root to the water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes
Strain the tea into a cup and add honey or lemon to taste (optional)
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with inflammation
preparations.preparation_study: According to "European journal of medicinal chemistry", Curcuma longa teas for inflammation have been reported to have therapeutic effects in human subjects, as demonstrated through various clinical trials assessing its anti-inflammatory properties.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Curcuma longa teas can help alleviate inflammation but may cause gastrointestinal side effects such as diarrhea, nausea, and stomach cramps in some individuals. To minimize potential side effects, it is essential to start with a low dose and gradually increase the amount, consume the tea with food, and avoid taking it on an empty stomach.
preparation_usage: Start by consulting a healthcare professional for guidance on using Curcuma longa teas.
Take 1 teaspoon of Curcuma longa tea powder in 1 cup of boiling water 2-3 times daily.
Drink tea immediately after preparing and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Continue for 7-14 days for initial inflammation relief and up to 3 months for chronic issues.
Monitor your symptoms and adjust dosage as needed under the guidance of a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Zingiber officinale teas, derived from the rhizome of the ginger plant, have been traditionally used to treat inflammation and various related ailments. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas are attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as gingerols and shogaols, which help to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. By consuming Zingiber officinale teas, individuals can experience relief from inflammation-related symptoms, including pain and swelling, due to the herbal preparation's ability to modulate the body's inflammatory response. The benefits of using Zingiber officinale teas to treat inflammation include reduced pain and discomfort, improved joint mobility, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh ginger root, a kettle, and a tea infuser.
Wash the ginger root and slice it into small pieces. Use 1 tablespoon of sliced ginger per cup of water.
Boil 1 cup of water in the kettle and pour it over the ginger in the tea infuser.
Let the tea steep for 5-7 minutes, depending on your desired strength.
Strain the tea into a cup and enjoy. You can add honey to taste, if needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Zingiber officinale teas for inflammation may be beneficial in treating inflammatory arthritis and diseases linked to persistent inflammation and pain, such as osteoarthritis (OA) and rheumatoid arthritis (RA).
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Zingiber officinale teas can be effective in treating inflammation, but may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea, especially when consumed in large quantities. Precautions should be taken to avoid interactions with other medications and to monitor blood sugar levels, as ginger can lower blood sugar levels and may not be suitable for individuals with diabetes.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare expert to confirm Zingiber officinale tea usage for inflammation.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Zingiber officinale root per cup of boiling water.
Drink 1-2 cups of Zingiber officinale tea per day, 2-3 times a week.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup if needed, after 1 week.
Monitor inflammation levels and adjust usage as recommended by the healthcare expert.

preparations.herb_desc: Echinacea purpurea teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation ailments, particularly due to their anti-inflammatory properties which help to reduce swelling and pain. The bioactive constituents of Echinacea purpurea, such as alkylamides, glycosides, and phenolic acids, contribute to its anti-inflammatory effects by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. By consuming Echinacea purpurea teas, individuals can experience relief from inflammation-related symptoms, including reduced joint pain and swelling, and improved immune function. The benefits of using Echinacea purpurea teas to treat inflammation ailments include reduced reliance on pharmaceutical medications, improved overall well-being, and a natural approach to managing chronic inflammation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Echinacea purpurea flowers, 1 cup of water, and a tea infuser or strainer.
Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers or 2 tablespoons of fresh flowers into the tea infuser or strainer.
Heat the water in a pot and bring it to a boil, then reduce the heat to a simmer.
Steep the Echinacea flowers in the water for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into a cup.
Drink the tea immediately, or let it cool before refrigerating it for later use.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "The Cochrane database of systematic reviews", Echinacea purpurea teas for inflammation may be beneficial, as the majority of available studies report positive results.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Echinacea purpurea teas can be used to treat inflammation, but some possible side effects include dizziness, nausea, and allergic reactions such as rash or itching, which may occur in individuals with sensitivities to the plant. To use Echinacea purpurea teas safely, take precautions like starting with small doses, avoiding consumption if pregnant or breastfeeding, and being cautious when combining it with other medications to treat inflammation.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional before using Echinacea purpurea teas for inflammation.
Take 1-2 cups of Echinacea purpurea tea daily, 2-3 times a week.
Start with a low dosage of 1 teaspoon of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Gradually increase dosage to 2 teaspoons per cup, if needed and under guidance.
Monitor inflammation levels and adjust dosage and frequency as advised by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Ginkgo biloba teas have been widely used to treat inflammation-related ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and terpenoids present in Ginkgo biloba teas, specifically quercetin, kaempferol, and bilobalide, help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and fibromyalgia. These bioactive constituents work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing oxidative stress, thereby providing relief from pain and discomfort. Regular consumption of Ginkgo biloba teas has been shown to be beneficial in treating inflammation-related ailments, offering an alternative to conventional medications and promoting overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Ginkgo biloba leaves.
Measure 1/4 cup of the leaves and place them in a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 2 cups of boiling water over the leaves.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid.
Drink 1 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Current molecular pharmacology", Ginkgo biloba teas for inflammation may be beneficial due to the presence of ginkgolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory properties by suppressing pro-inflammatory signaling cascades and oncogenic transcription factors in various preclinical models.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Ginkgo biloba teas can be used to treat inflammation, but possible side effects include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions such as itching or rashes. When using Ginkgo biloba teas to treat inflammation, take precautions to avoid excessive consumption, as high doses may cause bleeding or interact with blood-thinning medications, and also be cautious when taking it with other herbs or supplements to avoid potential interactions.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Ginkgo biloba tea 2 times a day.
Start with 1 teaspoon of dried Ginkgo biloba per cup.
Gradually increase to 2 teaspoons per cup as needed.
Drink tea 30 minutes before meals to reduce inflammation.
Use Ginkgo biloba tea for 2-4 weeks to see noticeable results.

preparations.herb_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation, an ailment characterized by pain, swelling, and redness in the body. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas are attributed to their ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, such as COX-2 and 5-LOX, which contribute to the inflammation process. The bioactive constituents of Glycyrrhiza glabra teas, including glycyrrhizin and flavonoids, help to reduce inflammation by modulating the immune response and scavenging free radicals. Regular consumption of these teas has been found to provide relief from inflammation-related conditions, such as arthritis and gout, by reducing pain and improving joint mobility.
preparation_procedure: Gather ingredients: 1 cup of water, 1 teaspoon of dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root, 1 tablespoon of honey (optional)
Boil the water and let it cool for 5 minutes. This will help the tea ingredients dissolve better
Add the dried Glycyrrhiza glabra root to the cooled water. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to release the active compounds
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or a tea strainer to remove the root particles. Discard the root
Add honey to taste, if desired. Drink the tea warm or at room temperature, 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Biomolecules", Glycyrrhiza glabra teas have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory activities, among other pharmacological activities, due to the presence of various phytoconstituents such as glycyrrhizin and other organic compounds.
preparations.preparation_side_effect:
Glycyrrhiza glabra teas can be effective in treating inflammation due to their anti-inflammatory properties; however, possible side effects may include headaches, dizziness, and an increased risk of potassium deficiency, particularly when consumed in excess or for extended periods.

To use Glycyrrhiza glabra teas safely, it is recommended to start with small doses and gradually increase as needed, avoid consuming them with other potassium-depleting substances, and monitor your blood pressure and potassium levels regularly.
preparation_usage: Drink 1 cup of Glycyrrhiza glabra tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried root per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes, then strain the tea.
Start with 1/2 cup of tea per day for 1-2 weeks, then increase to 1 cup.
Continue using for 2-6 weeks, as directed by a healthcare professional.

preparations.herb_desc: Camellia sinensis teas, derived from the leaves of the Camellia sinensis plant, have been traditionally used to treat inflammation and its associated ailments. The anti-inflammatory properties of these teas are attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and catechins, which help to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes. These herbal preparations have been shown to help treat inflammation by modulating the body's immune response and reducing oxidative stress, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain, swelling, and redness. Regular consumption of Camellia sinensis teas has been found to provide several benefits, including reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced antioxidant defenses.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried Camellia sinensis leaves (black, green, or white tea) and 1 cup of boiling water.
Heat 1 cup of water to 200°F (93°C) and pour it over the tea leaves in a cup.
Let the tea steep for 1-3 minutes, depending on desired strength and flavor.
Strain the tea leaves and discard them. Add 1 tablespoon of honey (optional) to sweeten the tea.
Drink the tea hot or cold, 2-3 times a day, to help reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Camellia sinensis teas for inflammation may offer benefits due to the presence of phenolic compounds, terpenoids, and fatty acids with associated anti-inflammatory activities.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Camellia sinensis teas can be used to treat inflammation due to their anti-inflammatory properties, but they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. Precautions should be taken when using Camellia sinensis teas to treat inflammation, including starting with a small dose, monitoring blood sugar levels in individuals with diabetes, and avoiding use in pregnant or breastfeeding women due to potential hormonal effects.
preparation_usage: Measure 1 teaspoon of Camellia sinensis tea leaves per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of tea per day for inflammation relief.
Start with a low dose (1 cup) and gradually increase as needed.
Continue usage for 2-4 weeks for optimal anti-inflammatory effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Lavandula angustifolia teas have been widely studied for their potential to treat inflammation and associated ailments due to their anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, in these teas possess strong anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to reduce inflammation and alleviate associated symptoms. By consuming Lavandula angustifolia teas, individuals can experience reduced pain and swelling, as well as improved overall well-being. The benefits of using Lavandula angustifolia teas to treat inflammation include reduced reliance on pharmaceuticals, minimal side effects, and a natural approach to managing symptoms.

Measure 1 tablespoon of dried flowers and place in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Heat 1 cup of boiling water and pour over the dried flowers.
Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the tea into another cup.

preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Lavandula angustifolia teas exhibit anti-inflammatory effects, which are attributed to the antioxidant properties of the plant's chemical composition.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Lavandula angustifolia teas can be used to treat inflammation, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications. To use Lavandula angustifolia teas safely, take precautions such as starting with small amounts, monitoring your body's response, and avoiding use if you have a history of allergies or sensitivities, and also be cautious when combining it with other herbal remedies or medications.
preparation_usage: Consult a healthcare professional for guidance on using Lavandula angustifolia tea for inflammation.
Drink 1 cup of Lavandula angustifolia tea 2-3 times a day as directed.
Use 1 teaspoon of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers per cup of boiling water.

Continue using Lavandula angustifolia tea for inflammation for 7-14 days as recommended.

preparations.herb_desc: Salvia officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation ailments due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain. The herbal preparation's ability to modulate the immune response and inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines contributes to its therapeutic effects. The bioactive constituents of Salvia officinalis, including rosmarinic acid, carnosic acid, and ursolic acid, possess potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help to alleviate inflammation and promote healing. Regular consumption of Salvia officinalis teas has been shown to offer benefits such as reduced inflammation, improved joint health, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves or 2 tablespoons of dried leaves.
Measure 1 cup of boiling water and pour it over the leaves in a heat-resistant cup or teapot.
Steep the leaves for 5-10 minutes to allow the flavors and oils to release.
Strain the tea using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a separate cup.
Drink the tea immediately, or store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to "Molecules (Basel, Switzerland)", Salvia officinalis teas for inflammation have shown promising anti-inflammatory properties, particularly the full flowering stage essential oil which exhibited a significant inhibitory effect on 5-lipoxygenase with an IC50 value of 9.24 ± 0.03 μg/mL.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salvia officinalis teas can be an effective herbal remedy for treating inflammation, but possible side effects may include digestive issues such as stomach upset and diarrhea due to its bitter compounds, as well as allergic reactions in rare cases. To safely use Salvia officinalis teas, it is recommended to start with a small dose and gradually increase as needed, and to avoid consuming it with other medications or substances that may interact with its active compounds, such as antacids, blood thinners, and sedatives.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Salvia officinalis tea for inflammation.
Drink 1 cup of Salvia officinalis tea, 3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Salvia officinalis leaves per cup.
Steep the tea for 5-7 minutes, then strain and drink.
Continue for 7-14 days, or as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Salix alba teas, a traditional herbal remedy, have been used to treat inflammation ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flavonoids and phenolic acids present in Salix alba teas help to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. By modulating the body's response to inflammation, Salix alba teas help to alleviate symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis, gout, and other inflammatory diseases. The benefits of using Salix alba teas to treat inflammation include reduced pain, swelling, and improved overall well-being, making it a valuable natural remedy for managing chronic inflammation.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Salix alba bark and 1 quart of water.
Boil the water in a large pot and add the dried Salix alba bark.
Reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes, then strain the mixture.
Discard the solids and let the tea cool to a comfortable temperature.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the study, Salix alba teas for inflammation are beneficial due to the presence of phytoconstituents such as steroids, glycosides, phenolics, flavonoids, alkaloids, polysaccharides, terpenoids, cannabinoids, and fatty acids, which can prevent undesirable inflammatory processes and exhibit anti-inflammatory activity.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Salix alba teas can be used to treat inflammation, but potential side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as stomach upset and diarrhea, due to its high salicylic acid content, as well as allergic reactions in some individuals. When using Salix alba teas, it is essential to take precautions, such as starting with small doses, monitoring blood pressure, and avoiding it if you have bleeding disorders, ulcers, or sensitive stomachs.
preparation_usage: Consult a doctor before using Salix alba tea for inflammation.
Drink 1-2 cups of Salix alba tea 2-3 times a day.
Use 1-2 teaspoons of dried Salix alba bark per cup of boiling water.
Steep for 5-10 minutes before straining and drinking.
Continue for 7-10 days or as directed by your doctor.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis teas, derived from the leaves of the rosemary plant, have been traditionally used to treat inflammation-related ailments due to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of rosemary, including carnosic acid and rosmarinic acid, play a crucial role in reducing inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and scavenging free radicals. These constituents help to alleviate symptoms associated with inflammation, such as pain, swelling, and redness, by modulating the body's inflammatory response and promoting relaxation. The benefits of using rosemary tea to treat inflammation include reduced discomfort, improved digestion, and enhanced overall well-being, making it a popular natural remedy for those seeking a chemical-free solution.
preparation_procedure: Gather 2 tablespoons of dried Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the dried leaves to the boiling water and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or cheesecloth.
Drink 1/2 cup of the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to the given study, Rosmarinus officinalis teas for inflammation have been evaluated in clinical and experimental studies, suggesting its potential anti-inflammatory effects.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosmarinus officinalis teas can be beneficial in reducing inflammation, however, they may cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and diarrhea in some individuals, particularly when consumed in large quantities. Precautions should be taken when using Rosmarinus officinalis teas to treat inflammation, including avoiding consumption in pregnant or breastfeeding women, as well as individuals with sensitive stomachs or allergies to the plant, and using it in moderation to minimize potential interactions with other medications.

preparations.herb_desc: Achillea millefolium teas, also known as yarrow tea, have been traditionally used to treat inflammation and its associated symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of this herbal preparation help to reduce pain and swelling by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. The bioactive constituents of yarrow tea, including sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, are responsible for its anti-inflammatory effects, which help to soothe and calm irritated tissues. Regular consumption of Achillea millefolium teas has been shown to provide relief from inflammation and promote overall well-being, making it a beneficial herbal remedy for those suffering from this ailment.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Achillea millefolium leaves and flowers. You can use dried ones too.
Use a tea infuser or a saucepan to steep 2-3 teaspoons of Achillea millefolium in 1 cup of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the solids. Let it cool down for a few minutes.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if needed. Achillea millefolium tea has a mild, earthy flavor.
Drink 1-2 cups of Achillea millefolium tea daily to help reduce inflammation. You can also store it in the fridge for later use.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Current pharmaceutical design, Achillea millefolium teas have been found to have anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its essential oil, proazulenes, sesquiterpene lactones, dicaffeoylquinic acids, and flavonoids, making them a potential natural remedy for inflammation.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Achillea millefolium teas can be used to treat inflammation ailments, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, allergic reactions, and interactions with certain medications, such as blood thinners. Precautions when using this herbal preparation include using it in moderation, avoiding its use during pregnancy and breastfeeding, and monitoring blood pressure and liver function in individuals with pre-existing conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Cinchona officinalis teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation ailments due to their analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The quinine content in these teas helps to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby alleviating symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis and gout. The bioactive constituents of Cinchona officinalis teas, including quinine, quinidine, and cinchonidine, contribute to their anti-inflammatory effects by modulating the body's response to injury and infection. The benefits of using Cinchona officinalis teas to treat inflammation include reduced pain and swelling, improved joint mobility, and a decrease in the risk of chronic inflammation-related diseases.

Strain the mixture into a cup using a piece of cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve.
Add 1 tablespoon of honey to the tea, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help with inflammation, as needed.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Saudi journal of gastroenterology : official journal of the Saudi Gastroenterology Association, Cinchona officinalis teas for inflammation have not been studied in this particular scientific study, as it actually focused on the healing effects of Calendula officinalis (also known as marigold) in experimentally induced ulcerative colitis in rats, not Cinchona officinalis.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Cinchona officinalis teas can be effective in treating inflammation, but it may also cause side effects such as stomach upset, nausea, and dizziness due to its high quinine content. To use Cinchona officinalis teas safely, it's essential to start with a low dose, gradually increasing it as needed, and to avoid using it for extended periods or in conjunction with other medications that may interact with its active compounds.

Precautions should be taken when using Silybum marianum teas, including avoiding large doses, monitoring liver function, and being cautious when combining it with medications like blood thinners, as it can enhance their effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Thymus vulgaris teas, also known as thyme tea, have been traditionally used to treat inflammation and its associated symptoms. The anti-inflammatory properties of thyme tea are attributed to its bioactive constituents, including thymol and carvacrol, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. Thyme tea helps to reduce inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby alleviating symptoms such as pain, redness, and swelling. The benefits of thyme tea in treating inflammation include its ability to soothe and calm irritated tissues, promote wound healing, and provide relief from conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory disorders.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 tablespoon of dried Thymus vulgaris leaves and 1 cup of boiling water.
Steep the Thymus vulgaris leaves in the boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired, and stir well.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Molecules (Basel, Switzerland), Thymus vulgaris teas for inflammation may be beneficial due to the presence of thymol, which exhibits high binding affinity with COX1 and COX2 inflammatory proteins, and the hexane-based extract of T. vulgaris shows high anti-inflammatory activity in a dose-response manner.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Thymus vulgaris teas can be used to treat inflammation ailments due to its anti-inflammatory properties, but it may cause side effects such as digestive issues, allergic reactions, and interactions with other medications. To use Thymus vulgaris teas safely, it is essential to take precautions such as starting with small amounts, monitoring blood pressure and blood sugar levels, and avoiding its use during pregnancy and breastfeeding.

preparations.herb_desc: Capsicum annuum teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation ailments due to their potent anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The bioactive constituents, including capsaicin, flavonoids, and carotenoids, possess antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that help to modulate the body's inflammatory response. Capsicum annuum teas work by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting healing. The benefits of using Capsicum annuum teas to treat inflammation ailments include reduced pain and discomfort, improved mobility, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh or dried Capsicum annuum (sweet pepper) leaves, 1 cup of water, and a heat-resistant cup.
Rinse the Capsicum annuum leaves with clean water, then chop them finely.
Combine the chopped Capsicum annuum leaves with 1 cup of water in the heat-resistant cup.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 10-15 minutes, then strain the liquid into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Journal of the American Nutrition Association, Capsicum annuum teas for inflammation do not exist as Capsicum annuum is the plant species that produces peppers, not tea, and the provided study does not mention Capsicum annuum teas.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Capsicum annuum teas can be used to treat inflammation, but possible side effects include stomach upset, heartburn, and allergic reactions, as well as interactions with certain medications such as blood thinners and diabetes medications. When using Capsicum annuum teas to treat inflammation, take precautions to avoid excessive consumption, which can lead to burning sensations in the mouth, throat, and digestive tract, and also consider reducing the frequency and amount of tea consumption to minimize potential side effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Withania somnifera teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation and its associated ailments due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The bioactive constituents, including withanolides, alkaloids, and glycosides, present in Withania somnifera teas, are responsible for their therapeutic effects, including their ability to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes. The consumption of Withania somnifera teas has been found to provide benefits in the treatment of inflammation, including reduced oxidative stress, improved immune function, and enhanced overall well-being. Regular consumption of Withania somnifera teas may also help to alleviate symptoms associated with chronic inflammation, such as joint pain and fatigue.
preparation_procedure: Gather Withania somnifera roots (1 cup) and a strainer.
Boil 1 cup of water in a pot.
Add the Withania somnifera roots to the boiling water.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture and drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Alternative medicine review : a journal of clinical therapeutic, Withania somnifera teas may offer a potential therapeutic modality for the treatment of inflammation by modulating cytokine secretion.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Withania somnifera teas can be used to treat inflammation, but possible side effects may include stomach upset, diarrhea, and allergic reactions in some individuals. To avoid adverse effects, it is recommended to consume Withania somnifera teas in moderation, start with small amounts, and avoid using them during pregnancy or breastfeeding, as well as in individuals with kidney or liver issues, as it may exacerbate these conditions.

preparations.herb_desc: Crocus sativus teas have been used to treat inflammation ailments due to their anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The herbal preparation is rich in bioactive constituents, including crocetin, safranal, and crocin, that have been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory mediators, thus helping to treat inflammation. These bioactive compounds help to scavenge free radicals, reduce oxidative stress, and modulate the immune response, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms. The benefits of using Crocus sativus teas to treat inflammation include reduced pain and swelling, improved joint mobility, and enhanced overall well-being.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of water and 1 tablespoon of dried Crocus sativus flowers.
Place the flowers in a heat-proof cup or teapot.
Heat the water to 212°F (100°C) and pour it over the flowers.
Steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the mixture into another cup.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help reduce inflammation.
preparations.preparation_study: N/A
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Crocus sativus teas can be used to treat inflammation, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal upset, dizziness, and allergic reactions such as skin rashes or itching. To minimize potential risks, it is essential to start with a low dose, gradually increase as needed, and be cautious when consuming the tea with other medications, especially blood thinners, as it may enhance their effects.

preparations.herb_desc: Rosa rugosa teas have been traditionally used to treat inflammation, particularly in Asian medicine, where they are valued for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The anti-inflammatory effects of Rosa rugosa teas are attributed to the presence of bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain. By inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, Rosa rugosa teas help to reduce inflammation and promote healing in the affected areas. The benefits of using Rosa rugosa teas to treat inflammation include reduced pain and swelling, improved wound healing, and a lower risk of infection.
preparation_procedure: Gather 1 cup of dried Rosa rugosa flowers. You can buy them online or at a local herbal store.
Measure 1 tablespoon of the dried flowers and add them to a teapot or a heat-resistant cup.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the flowers. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup and discard the flowers. Add honey or lemon to taste, if desired.
Drink the tea 2-3 times a day for inflammation relief. Store any leftover tea in the fridge for up to 24 hours.
preparations.preparation_study: According to Biomolecules & therapeutics, Rosa rugosa teas for inflammation have been found to significantly inhibit the production of inflammatory mediators such as nitric oxide, prostaglandin E2, tumor necrosis factor, and interleukins, as well as the activity of mitogen-activated protein kinases and nuclear factor-kappa B signaling pathway.
preparations.preparation_side_effect: Rosa rugosa teas can be used to treat inflammation, but possible side effects may include gastrointestinal issues, such as nausea and stomach upset, due to its high polyphenol content. Precautions should be taken to avoid excessive consumption, as it may also interact with certain medications and exacerbate conditions like high blood pressure, kidney problems, or allergies.
